name: Jenny Boavista
technical_background: Program and operations management across AI consulting, government healthcare, and clinical trials. PMP certified with MBA in Healthcare Management. Drives operational excellence at an AI insights firm including client success, GenAI training program coordination, and conference event strategy. Strong in formal project management (WBS, Gantt charts, risk analysis, communication plans) and contract administration. Uses ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, GitHub Copilot, Midjourney, and has used OpenAI/Anthropic APIs. Basic scripting background (Excel, simple automation). No deep coding or ML engineering experience.

current_profile.summary: Program and Operations Manager at GAI Insights, an AI consulting firm, with 8 years of experience spanning AI operations, government healthcare policy, clinical trials operations, and independent PM consulting. Drives operational excellence across client success, event planning, and Generative AI enablement programs. Coordinates the design and delivery of GenAI training programs for internal teams and external clients. Organizes the Women in AI Breakfast and supports the GAI Insights Annual Conference. Previously managed pharmacy Medicaid operations at Oregon Health Authority and clinical DXA operations at Bioclinica. PMP certified with MBA in Healthcare Management. Uses ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini regularly for project management tasks. Strong in stakeholder communication, process optimization, and cross-functional collaboration but has no hands-on experience building AI systems, agents, or technical platform features.
technical_skills: Project management (PMP: WBS, Gantt charts, risk analysis, communication plans)
Training program design and delivery coordination
AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Copilot, Midjourney)
OpenAI API and Anthropic API usage
soft_skills: Stakeholder communication across technical and non-technical audiences
AI community building (Women in AI Breakfast)
current_profile.ai_experience: Works at GAI Insights, an AI consulting firm, where she coordinates GenAI training programs and champions AI tool adoption for internal processes. Uses ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini regularly for project management tasks including drafting documents, ideation, and workflow support. Has used GitHub Copilot for coding assistance and Midjourney for creative work. Familiar with OpenAI and Anthropic APIs. Familiar with the AI tool landscape from working alongside AI practitioners and subject matter experts daily. Has not built, trained, or evaluated AI/ML models. No experience with agents, RAG systems, prompt engineering frameworks, or production AI deployment.

learning_intent: Wants to transition into an AI Operations Manager role leading the design, automation, and optimization of business processes using AI systems and workflow automation tools. Primary learning goals: (1) Understand AI concepts and terminology, (2) Use AI tools for productivity, (3) Lead AI initiatives at work, (4) Research and experiment with cutting-edge AI. Aims to gain hands-on expertise with MCP frameworks, agent orchestration, RAG systems, and automation platforms (n8n, Zapier, Make) while leveraging existing strengths in operational excellence, governance, stakeholder management, and AI enablement training.

target_jd.title: AI Operations Manager
requirements: Architect, deploy, and maintain AI-driven workflows to streamline operations across teams
Implement automations using n8n, Zapier, Make, and custom integrations leveraging APIs and webhooks
Build and maintain AI agent pipelines using MCP, vector databases, and orchestration frameworks
Translate business requirements into efficient, scalable automation solutions
Manage internal AI tooling ecosystem, including LLM applications, automation platforms, retrieval systems, and agent orchestration tools
Evaluate new AI tools and recommend adoption based on ROI, technical feasibility, and operational impact
Monitor system performance, troubleshoot failures, and optimize reliability of AI-driven processes
Establish KPIs and reporting dashboards to quantify operational impact of automation initiatives
Implement governance frameworks for safe and compliant AI usage
Partner with IT and Security teams to maintain data integrity, access controls, and auditability within AI systems
Lead training sessions and internal workshops on AI tools, automation best practices, and workflow design
Enable business teams to self-serve through standardized AI and automation playbooks

expected_skill_gaps.description: Critical gap. No experience building AI agents. Needs MCP protocol concepts, tool definitions, single-agent loops, error handling, multi-agent patterns, and guardrails to build and maintain AI agent pipelines for workflow automation.
Critical gap. No RAG experience. Needs RAG fundamentals, hybrid retrieval, and chunking strategies to work with vector databases (Pinecone, Weaviate, Chroma) and embedding-based retrieval systems.
Moderate gap. Basic scripting only (Excel automation). Needs REST API basics for webhook/API integrations and Python basics for custom automation scripting.
Gap. No evaluation or observability experience. Needs tracing and observability for monitoring AI-driven processes, and quality dashboards for establishing KPIs and reporting on automation impact.

Transferable policy background (Oregon Health Authority, federal compliance) but needs AI-specific governance depth. Needs policy-to-controls mapping to implement governance frameworks for safe and compliant AI usage.
Gap. No AI security experience. Needs data leakage prevention to partner with IT and Security teams on data integrity and access controls within AI systems.
Gap. No production AI operations experience. Needs latency drivers and optimization to monitor system performance and optimize reliability of AI-driven processes.
Gap. Needs embeddings and semantic similarity knowledge as a prerequisite for RAG systems and vector database work.
